jobs.title: Head Cheerleading Coach & Program Director
jobs.description:
**Head Cheerleading Coach \& Program Director**

Location: Yonkers, NY

Position Type: Part\-Time (Year\-Round)  
Approximately 10\-20\+ hours per week depending on season, with opportunity for growth. Starting Pay: $35/hour DOE

Required Schedule: Tuesdays \& Thursdays 5:00\-8:00pm, Saturdays 9:00am\-4:00pm, plus additional remote administrative work and occasional weekend events.

**About New York All Stars**

New York All Stars Fitness \& Performing Arts is one of Westchester County's fastest\-growing youth enrichment organizations. Our mission is to build confident, resilient, and creative young people through competitive cheerleading, performing arts, fitness, leadership, and community.

We're looking for someone who shares that mission.

This is more than a coaching position. We're searching for an inspiring leader who will help shape the future of our cheerleading program while becoming an integral member of our leadership team.

New York All Stars is not a traditional All Star program \- in fact, our programming extends far beyond just cheerleading and includes a multitude of fitness and performing arts opportunities. We've intentionally built a program that blends the athletic excellence of competitive cheerleading with the community, leadership, and inclusive spirit often found in school\-based programs. We believe every athlete deserves high\-quality coaching in an environment that prioritizes teamwork, character, confidence, and belonging as much as competitive success.

**Position Overview**

The Head Cheerleading Coach \& Program Director will oversee our All Star Cheerleading Program, leading practices, preparing teams for competitions, mentoring assistant coaches, and helping families feel welcomed and supported.The ideal candidate believes that success is measured by far more than trophies. They value sportsmanship, teamwork, confidence, accountability, and creating an environment where every athlete can thrive.

**Responsibilities**

* Lead weekly practices for multiple cheer teams, ages 4\-16\.
* Oversee tumbling instructional programs
* Choreograph and clean routines appropriate for each team's level.
* Prepare athletes for competitions throughout the season.
* Teach proper stunt progressions, jumps, tumbling technique, motions, dance, conditioning, and performance skills.
* Mentor, train, and evaluate coaches.
* Foster a positive, encouraging, and disciplined team culture.
* Communicate professionally with families regarding schedules, expectations, and athlete progress.
* Assist with athlete evaluations, placements, and recruiting.
* Coordinate practices, competitions, showcases, and special events.
* Collaborate with the Executive Director to continue growing and improving the program.
* Support fundraising initiatives, community outreach, and promotional events.
* Represent New York All Stars with professionalism both inside and outside the gym.

**Qualifications**

* Cheerleading coaching experience required.
* Strong knowledge of stunting, tumbling progressions, choreography, and safety standards.
* Experience working with children and teens.
* Excellent leadership and communication skills.
* Organized, dependable, and positive.
* Ability to motivate athletes while maintaining high expectations.
* CPR/First Aid certification (or willingness to obtain).

**We're looking for someone who...**

* Loves coaching kids as much as winning.
* Wants to help build a program, not simply coach a team.
* Believes character development is just as important as athletic development.
* Enjoys collaborating with families and fellow coaches.
* Is excited to become a long\-term leader within a growing organization.

**What We Offer**

* Starting pay of $35/hour based on experience.
* 401K Enrollment
* Paid training and curriculum support.
* Year\-round leadership opportunities.
* Professional development.
* A collaborative and supportive team environment.
* The opportunity to make a lasting impact on young athletes while helping shape the future of one of Westchester's fastest\-growing youth programs.
* Provide coaching instruction in cheerleading fundamentals, stunts, tumbling, dance, and team coordination
* Manage behavior and discipline to foster a respectful environment that encourages teamwork and sportsmanship
* Collaborate with other sports coaches to integrate cross\-training opportunities such as gymnastics or martial arts for skill enhancement
* Ensure all activities comply with safety protocols; operate AED (Automated External Defibrillator) devices and administer first aid or CPR when necessary
* Communicate regularly with parents, guardians, and school staff to provide updates on team progress and upcoming events
* Maintain detailed records of team attendance, performance evaluations, and safety incidents
* Promote health club standards by encouraging physical fitness, proper hydration, and injury prevention strategies

**Skills**

* Proven experience in cheerleading coaching or sports team leadership with a focus on youth development
* Strong background in sports\-specific training instruction including stunts, tumbling, dance, and choreography
* Knowledge of martial arts such as karate or judo enhances stunt safety and coordination skills
* Experience working with children or students in educational or recreational settings
* Excellent communication skills for effective client (parent/guardian) engagement and team motivation
* Ability to teach physical education concepts while managing behavior positively
* Certifications in first aid, CPR (Cardiopulmonary Resuscitation), AED operation, and child safety protocols are highly desirable
* Familiarity with soccer coaching or basketball can support versatile athletic training approaches
* Demonstrated leadership in sports coaching environments with a focus on teamwork and skill development
* Experience in health club or gym settings is a plus for understanding fitness fundamentals

Join us as we cultivate a spirited cheerl

jobs.title: Senior Director of Development
jobs.description:
**Senior Director of Development**
==================================


We are seeking an accomplished and results\-driven **Senior Director of Development** to lead the organization's fundraising, business development, and revenue\-generation strategy.


This senior leadership position is responsible for expanding and diversifying funding through corporate partnerships, major gifts, grants, sponsorships, and national and global initiatives. The Senior Director of Development will exercise a high level of independent judgment in identifying revenue opportunities, cultivating relationships, negotiating partnerships, and developing strategic initiatives that advance the organization's mission and long\-term financial sustainability.


The ideal candidate is a strategic relationship builder with a demonstrated record of securing significant funding, developing strong donor and corporate partnerships, and leading revenue\-focused development efforts within a nonprofit or mission\-driven organization.

**We offer the following competitive benefits package:**

* Competitive Salary based on experience
* Medical Benefits
* Paid Time Off
* Paid Holidays
* Retirement Plan

**Why Join Alma Domestic Violence Foundation?**
===============================================


At **Alma Domestic Violence Foundation (ADVF)**, we believe every individual deserves to live free from violence, fear, and abuse. Our mission is to empower survivors of domestic violence by providing comprehensive advocacy, supportive services, housing resources, legal assistance, education, and community partnerships that promote safety, healing, and long\-term independence. As our **Senior Director of Development**, you will play a pivotal role in advancing this mission by securing the financial resources and strategic partnerships needed to expand our programs and increase our impact. Your leadership will directly support innovative initiatives that improve the lives of survivors and strengthen communities throughout Metro Atlanta and beyond. This is more than a fundraising position\-it is an opportunity to shape the future of a growing nonprofit organization, cultivate transformational partnerships, and help build sustainable funding that allows ADVF to serve more individuals and families in need. If you are a visionary leader with a passion for philanthropy, community engagement, and creating lasting social change, we invite you to join our team and make a meaningful difference.

**Position Overview**
=====================

**Fundraising and Revenue Strategy**
====================================

* Develop and execute a comprehensive development and revenue strategy aligned with the organization's mission, strategic priorities, and financial goals.
* Identify, cultivate, solicit, and secure funding from corporations, foundations, government entities, major donors, and other funding partners.
* Lead sales\-oriented fundraising strategies designed to generate sustainable revenue and expand organizational impact.
* Establish annual fundraising goals, performance benchmarks, and revenue projections.
* Analyze fundraising performance and adjust strategies as needed to meet financial targets.

**Business Development and Strategic Partnerships**
===================================================

* Build and manage a robust pipeline of prospective donors, sponsors, corporate partners, and funding organizations.
* Lead partnership discussions, sponsorship proposals, and funding negotiations.
* Establish and maintain strategic relationships with corporate, philanthropic, governmental, and community partners.
* Identify opportunities to expand the organization's presence through national and international partnerships and initiatives.
* Represent the organization at conferences, networking events, donor meetings, community engagements, and other professional forums.

**Donor Engagement and Stewardship**
====================================

* Develop and oversee donor cultivation, solicitation, recognition, engagement, and stewardship strategies.
* Strengthen relationships with existing donors while identifying opportunities to increase giving and long\-term engagement.
* Ensure donors and funding partners receive timely communication, impact updates, recognition, and reporting.
* Support the development of compelling funding proposals, sponsorship materials, presentations, and donor communications.

**Grant and Funding Compliance**
================================

* Collaborate with executive leadership and program teams to align funding opportunities with organizational and programmatic priorities.
* Ensure compliance with donor requirements, grant conditions, contractual obligations, and reporting deadlines.
* Monitor the appropriate use of restricted funds and support accurate financial and programmatic reporting.
* Maintain organized records of funding opportunities, donor interactions, proposals, awards, and reporting requirements.

**Leadership and Team Management**
==================================

* Provide strategic direction and leadership to development staff, consultants, volunteers, and other assigned personnel.
* Establish performance expectations, monitor progress, and promote accountability for development goals.
* Support the professional development and effectiveness of the development team.
* Foster a collaborative, ethical, and results\-oriented fundraising culture across the organization.

**Required Qualifications**
===========================

* Bachelor's degree in business, nonprofit management, communications, public administration, marketing, or a related field.
* At least 7–10 years of progressive experience in fundraising, business development, corporate partnerships, nonprofit development, or sales leadership.
* Demonstrated success securing major gifts, corporate sponsorships, grants, or other significant funding.
* Proven experience building and managing donor or business development pipelines.
* St

jobs.title: Chief Development Officer
jobs.description:
**Chief Development Officer**

*Lead philanthropy, engagement, and community impact across coastal Georgia*

**Location:** Savannah, Georgia

**Salary Range:** $120,000–$140,000 annually

**Position Type:** Full\-time, Executive Leadership

**Reports to:** President/CEO

**About Second Harvest of Coastal Georgia**

As a proud member of the Feeding America network, Second Harvest of Coastal Georgia is the leading hunger\-relief organization serving 21 counties in our region. With facilities in Savannah and Brunswick, we provide more than 26 million pounds of food each year to over 250 local charitable agencies and programs, including food pantries, shelters, and schools.

Through innovative programs like Kids Cafe, Mobile Food Pantry, and Senior Meals—and in partnership with community organizations and volunteers—Second Harvest is dedicated to nourishing lives, building community resilience, and eliminating food insecurity.

**Position Summary**

Second Harvest of Coastal Georgia is seeking an accomplished and mission\-driven Chief Development Officer to lead the organization’s fundraising, marketing and communications, volunteer engagement, advocacy, and community outreach strategies.

As a key member of the executive leadership team, the Chief Development Officer will provide the strategic vision needed to expand philanthropic support, strengthen community relationships, elevate Second Harvest’s visibility, and ensure long\-term financial sustainability. Reporting directly to the President/CEO and working closely with the Board of Directors, this leader will develop and execute comprehensive annual and multi\-year development plans supporting the organization’s mission and strategic priorities.

The ideal candidate is an experienced fundraising executive, collaborative team leader, compelling communicator, and skilled relationship builder who is energized by the opportunity to make a meaningful difference throughout coastal Georgia.

**Executive Leadership and Strategy**

· Partner with the President/CEO and executive leadership team to establish and advance long\-range organizational goals and strategic priorities.

· Develop annual and multi\-year fundraising and engagement plans with measurable goals and performance indicators.

· Serve as a strategic advisor on philanthropy, communications, volunteer engagement, advocacy, and community relations.

· Monitor philanthropic trends, community needs, economic conditions, and donor behavior to identify opportunities for innovation and sustainable growth.

**Fundraising and Resource Development**

· Lead a comprehensive fundraising program encompassing major gifts, annual giving, direct mail, corporate partnerships, foundation and institutional giving, government grants, planned giving, capital campaigns, special events, and donor stewardship.

· Cultivate, solicit, and steward major donors, corporate sponsors, foundations, and community partners.

· Diversify revenue streams to strengthen organizational sustainability and reduce funding risk.

· Develop compelling cases for support and engagement strategies that improve donor acquisition, retention, and lifetime value.

· Establish effective systems for prospect development, donor recognition, stewardship, and fundraising performance measurement.

· Ensure compliance with applicable legal, ethical, and professional fundraising standards.

**Marketing, Communications, and Brand Leadership**

· Oversee integrated marketing and communications strategies that strengthen Second Harvest’s visibility, reputation, and community impact.

· Direct public relations, digital marketing, social media, storytelling, traditional media, and direct\-response campaigns.

· Guide the development of annual reports, newsletters, impact reports, press releases, campaign materials, and executive communications.

· Serve as an organizational spokesperson and represent Second Harvest at community events, public presentations, stakeholder meetings, and media opportunities.

**Advocacy and Community Engagement**

· Partner with the President/CEO and community leaders to increase awareness of hunger, food insecurity, and related public\-policy issues.

· Build relationships with elected officials, government agencies, nonprofit organizations, corporate partners, civic groups, and advocacy coalitions.

· Develop strategies that educate and engage donors, volunteers, and community stakeholders around hunger\-relief priorities.

· Provide strategic oversight of volunteer services, ensuring meaningful experiences that deepen community involvement and expand organizational capacity.

· Create opportunities for volunteers and community partners to become long\-term supporters, ambassadors, and advocates for Second Harvest’s mission.

**Board and Team Leadership**

· Collaborate with the Board of Directors, Advisory Board, and Development and Communications Committee to advance fundraising and engagement priorities.

· Equip board members with the strategies, information, and tools needed to cultivate relationships and serve as effective organizational ambassadors.

· Lead, mentor, and develop high\-performing development, marketing, communications, and volunteer engagement teams.

· Promote a culture of collaboration, accountability, innovation, continuous improvement, and professional growth.

· Establish clear performance expectations and provide employees with ongoing coaching, feedback, and recognition.

**Financial and Operational Management**

· Develop and manage departmental budgets with a strong commitment to responsible stewardship.

· Use fundraising analytics and performance data to evaluate effectiveness, guide decisions, and improve return on investment.

· Identify opportunities to strengthen technology, systems, and operational processes.

**Qualifications, Education, and Experience**

· Bachelor’s degree required; master’s degree or Certified Fund Raising Executive designation preferred.

· At least 10 yea

jobs.title: Executive Director
jobs.description:
**About Us**

Latimer House is a nonprofit historic house living museum dedicated to preserving and sharing the history and culture of our community. Through tours and special events, we connect visitors of all ages with our region's unique heritage.

**Position Summary**

The Executive Director serves as the museum's chief executive and is responsible for overall leadership, administration, financial management, fundraising, and community engagement. Reporting to the Board of Directors, the Executive Director works closely with staff, volunteers, donors, and community partners to advance the museum's mission and ensure long\-term sustainability.

**Key Responsibilities**

* Lead the day\-to\-day operations of the museum.
* Develop and manage the annual budget.
* Oversee financial reporting and organizational compliance.
* Build relationships with donors, members, sponsors, and community partners.
* Lead fundraising efforts, including grants, corporate and individual donations and special events.
* Supervise staff and volunteers.
* Work with the Board of Directors on strategic planning and governance.
* Oversee museum tours, exhibits, create, implement and oversee programming, and visitor experience.
* Ensure proper care of museum facilities and collections.
* Represent the museum at community events and public meetings.

**Qualifications**

Required:

* Bachelor's degree or equivalent experience.
* Experience with CRM.
* Experience managing budgets and personnel.
* Strong written and verbal communication skills.
* Demonstrated fundraising or grant\-writing experience.
* Ability to work collaboratively with volunteers and community stakeholders.

Preferred:

* Experience in museums, historic sites, or cultural organizations.
* Knowledge of nonprofit governance.
* Experience with membership programs and donor relations.
* Familiarity with collections management or public programming.

**Ideal Candidate**

We're looking for a collaborative leader who is organized, adaptable, and passionate about local history. The ideal candidate enjoys wearing many hats, solving problems creatively, and building relationships throughout the community.

**To Apply**

Please submit:

* Cover letter
* Resume
* Three professional references

Resumes will be reviewed beginning 7/20/2026 and will remain open until the position is filled.

**Latimer House / Lower Cape Fear Historical Society, Inc. is an Equal Opportunity Employer.**

Pay: From $40,000\.00 per year

Work Location: In person

jobs.title: Executive Director
jobs.description:
GO! Transportation Executive Director

GENERAL INFORMATION:

The Executive Director position is a full\-time salaried position. The position will be responsible for leading GO! Transportation to becoming a successful nonprofit entity. The ED reports to the Board of Directors and its committees, and is responsible for overall strategic and operational administration, including developing and implementing procedures and policies.

RESPONSIBILITIES:

ORGANIZATIONAL LEADERSHIP

· Build GO! into an organization that is recognized as a premier provider of transportation services for youth by embracing a spirit of collaboration and cooperation.

· Direct, coordinate, and support the organization’s services in accordance with the goals and policies established by the Board of Directors.

· Recommend policies and procedures to the board, ensuring they align with organizational goals and regulatory requirements for effective governance and decision\-making.

· Together with the board president, plan and execute board meetings and board development activities. Assist board committees as needed. Attend meetings of the Board of Directors and prepare meeting minutes.

· Drive initiatives that contribute to short\- and long\-term organizational planning and operational excellence.

DEVELOPMENT \& OPERATIONS

· Administer general operations of the organization, maintain office management and recordkeeping systems, and ensure that financial accountability is achieved.

· Develop and manage annual budgets and identify short and long\-term financial support.

· Review and monitor monthly financial reports and expenditures while developing, maintaining, and overseeing all fundraising and accounting systems and procedures to support the long\-term financial success of the organization.

· Work with our fiscal sponsor to administer payroll and employee benefits.

· Initiate/monitor all organizational insurance and policies.

· Serve as liaison with all outside vendors. Establish, support, and optimize a contractual relationship with transportation providers and assure integrity in its delivery of service.

· Maintain up\-to\-date knowledge of transportation regulations and compliance requirements. Develop/monitor policies and procedures that assure the safety of all youth riders.

· Promote public awareness of GO! as the primary spokesperson, making presentations, preparing and disseminating marketing materials, and maintaining online presence.

PROGRAMMING

· Observe program activities to ensure safety and effectiveness.

· Develop, monitor, and maintain all agreements and community partnerships with youth\-serving agencies served by GO!

· Chaperone and coordinate the route as needed. Develop and oversee the management of the route, ensuring smooth implementation and efficient operation while addressing any challenges that arise.

· Hire and supervise the staff team, coordinate professional development opportunities, and hold regular staff meetings and performance reviews.

QUALIFICATIONS \& REQUIREMENTS:

· Successful experience working with youth and families from various backgrounds, and a high comfort level working in a diverse environment.

· Demonstrated leadership and vision in major projects or initiatives.

· Excellent interpersonal and communication skills (both oral and written) and a collaborative management style.

· High school diploma or GED required. BA preferred, or two years of experience working in an administrative position.

· Proficiency in Excel, Word, and Google Workspace (including Google Voice), or ability to quickly learn new applications and technologies.

PHYSICAL AND MENTAL DEMANDS:

· Mobility around the program facility and various locations within the community to meet with staff, families, and community partners.

· Ability to lift and carry materials and/or equipment for up to 30 pounds.

· Ability to maintain mental focus while working at a desk and when attending meetings for extended periods.

PRE\-EMPLOYMENT REQUIREMENTS:

· Valid driver's license and proof of insurance.

· Successful completion of a background check and drug screen that meets requirements.

COMPENSATION:

● Salary position, commensurate with experience.

*Individuals who feel they are well\-suited for this position, but who do not meet all requirements are still encouraged to apply.* 

Pay: From $50,000\.00 per year

Work Location: In person

jobs.title: Executive Director
jobs.description:
Executive Director

The Islamic Center of Wheaton (ICW) seeks an experienced, mission\-driven **Executive Director \& Director of Administrative Affairs** to provide leadership and oversight of the organization’s operations, administration, fundraising, community engagement, educational initiatives, and strategic development.

The Executive Director will work closely with the Board of Directors, committees, staff, volunteers, and community stakeholders to advance ICW’s mission, strengthen organizational effectiveness, support sustainable growth, and foster a welcoming and vibrant Islamic environment.

This position requires a strategic leader with demonstrated experience in nonprofit management, fundraising, financial oversight, team leadership, community development, and organizational administration.

**Key Responsibilities:Organizational Leadership**

* Support and advance ICW’s mission, vision, and strategic objectives.
* Collaborate with the President and Board of Directors to implement organizational priorities.
* Provide regular operational updates, recommendations, and reports to leadership.
* Develop and implement policies, procedures, and operational improvements.
* Promote organizational excellence, accountability, and continuous improvement.

**Fundraising \& Development (Critical Function)**

* Lead and support fundraising initiatives to strengthen ICW’s long\-term sustainability.
* Partner with fundraising committees to develop annual fundraising strategies and campaigns.
* Build and maintain relationships with donors, community leaders, sponsors, and supporters.
* Assist in securing grants, sponsorships, endowments (Waqf), major gifts, and planned giving opportunities.
* Support Friday, Ramadan, and special fundraising events, including donor stewardship and pledge follow\-up.
* Develop strategies to diversify revenue sources and strengthen long\-term financial stability.
* Ensure fundraising initiatives are effectively executed by staff and volunteers.

**Operations Management**

* Oversee day\-to\-day operations of ICW facilities and affiliated educational programs.
* Supervise office administration and operational staff.
* Coordinate vendor relationships and municipal interactions.
* Ensure facility maintenance, safety, and operational readiness.
* Manage organizational records and administrative processes.

**Financial Management**

* Work closely with the Treasurer and Finance Committee on budgeting and financial oversight.
* Assist in maintaining fiscal discipline and responsible resource utilization.
* Support long\-term financial planning and sustainability efforts.
* Monitor operational expenditures and organizational efficiency.

**Human Resources \& Team Leadership**

* Assist with recruitment, hiring, onboarding, supervision, and retention of staff.
* Foster a positive workplace culture and encourage professional development.
* Coordinate staffing plans and operational coverage.
* Support volunteer recruitment, engagement, and recognition efforts.

**Programs \& Community Development**

* Coordinate educational, religious, family, youth, and community programs.
* Support interfaith initiatives and community outreach efforts.
* Work collaboratively with committees to implement organizational programming.
* Promote meaningful engagement among members and community stakeholders.

**Youth \& Student Development**

* Develop and support youth mentorship and leadership programs.
* Create opportunities for youth participation in ICW activities, committees, and leadership development.
* Provide mentorship and career guidance to students and young professionals.
* Encourage youth involvement in community service, civic engagement, and personal development initiatives.

**Educational Support Initiatives**

* Support tutoring, academic assistance, and student enrichment programs.
* Collaborate with families, educators, and local schools to promote student success.
* Facilitate partnerships with educational institutions and community stakeholders.
* Promote programs that integrate academic excellence with ethical, spiritual, and personal growth.

**Religious Support Responsibilities**

* Support religious programming and work collaboratively with ICW Imams and the Religious Affairs Committee.
* Assist with religious activities and community engagement efforts consistent with ICW’s mission.
* Ability to lead daily prayers, weekend prayers, or deliver Khutbah when needed, appropriate, and qualified.

**Governance \& Conflict Resolution**

* Assist leadership and committees in addressing operational challenges and resolving concerns.
* Promote transparency, collaboration, and organizational alignment.
* Ensure adherence to ICW bylaws, policies, and governance standards.

**Qualifications Required**

* Bachelor’s degree or equivalent leadership experience.
* Minimum of five (5\) years of experience in nonprofit management, operations, fundraising, educational administration, community leadership, or executive management.
* Demonstrated success in fundraising and donor development.
* Strong organizational, communication, and leadership skills.
* Experience with budgeting, financial oversight, and organizational planning.
* Ability to manage multiple priorities within a dynamic, community\-centered environment.

**Preferred**

* Experience working within Islamic nonprofit organizations.
* Familiarity with Masjid operations, Islamic institutions, and nonprofit governance.
* Experience leading fundraising campaigns and donor engagement initiatives.
* Ability to provide religious support, including leading prayers or delivering Khutbah when appropriate and qualified.

**Compensation \& Benefits**

* Competitive compensation package commensurate with qualifications and experience.
* Paid vacation and leave benefits.
* Professional development and continuing education opportunities.
* Opportunity to serve and help shape the future growth of one of the region’s leading Islamic centers

jobs.title: Director of Development
jobs.description:
**Director of Development**  

**Goddard House \| Brookline, Massachusetts**
  

**Position Summary**  

Goddard House, a nonprofit leader in innovative approaches to healthy aging, seeks a dynamic and relationship\-driven Director of Development to lead and grow its philanthropic program. This is a newly created position designed to expand Goddard House’s philanthropic impact and strengthen a culture of philanthropy across the organization.
Reporting directly to the President \& CEO, this role offers a unique opportunity to develop and implement a comprehensive fundraising strategy that supports both the Goddard House assisted living community and the continued expansion of Creative Aging Connections, a Goddard House initiative that advances creative arts engagement and healthy aging for under\-resourced older adults living in Boston and surrounding neighborhoods.
  

The Director of Development will have responsibility for the organization’s fundraising strategy and results, while overseeing key advancement functions that support donor engagement and philanthropic growth. This role is both strategic and hands\-on, requiring the ability to develop long\-term fundraising strategy while actively managing donor relationships and driving near\-term results.
Working closely with the President \& CEO and Board of Trustees, the Director will strengthen relationships with donors and community partners and expand philanthropic investment in Goddard House’s mission to support purpose, autonomy, and choice as we age.
  

**Leadership \& Responsibilities**  

**Strategic Leadership**
* Partner with the President \& CEO to develop and implement a comprehensive development strategy and foster a culture of philanthropy.
* Develop and lead annual fundraising goals and strategies supporting programmatic priorities and capital and facility\-related initiatives.
* Collaborate with senior leadership to articulate compelling opportunities for philanthropic support.

**Fundraising and Donor Engagement**
* Identify, cultivate, solicit, and steward individual donors, foundations, and corporate partners, building and managing a robust pipeline of prospects.
* Manage a personal portfolio of major donors and prospects, building long\-term relationships and securing significant philanthropic gifts.
* Lead major gift strategy and partner with the CEO and Board of Trustees to cultivate and solicit significant gifts.
* Expand the donor base by developing new opportunities for philanthropic engagement and making targeted asks across all giving levels.
* Represent Goddard House at donor events and community outreach activities.

**Brand, Storytelling \& Visibility**
* Advance Creative Aging Connections (CAC) brand and storytelling to support fundraising and donor engagement; collaborate with the marketing team to align messaging.
* Strengthen Goddard House and CAC’s visibility through events, campaigns, partnerships, and public engagement opportunities.
* Collaborate with program and leadership teams to ensure consistent messaging about mission and impact.

**Board Engagement**
* Support the Board of Trustees and Development Committee in advancing fundraising goals.
* Partner with board members to identify, engage and cultivate new donor prospects.
* Provide regular reports on fundraising progress and donor engagement.

**Development Operations \& Infrastructure**
* Oversee donor database management, gift tracking, and development reporting.
* Ensure effective stewardship and acknowledgment practices.
* Track key metrics including donor retention, acquisition, and revenue performance.
* Use fundraising data and donor analytics to inform strategy, forecasting, and performance tracking.
* Provide leadership and oversight for staff and external partners, including grant writers and consultants.

**First\-Year Priorities**  

During the first year, the Director of Development will focus on strengthening the organization’s fundraising foundation while beginning to expand philanthropic support.  

**Key priorities include:**
* Developing and implementing a comprehensive fundraising strategy in partnership with the President \& CEO.
* Expanding the donor pipeline and increasing philanthropic support for Creative Aging Connections.
* Cultivating relationships with residents, current and former families, and community supporters.
* Strengthening fundraising infrastructure, systems, and reporting organization\-wide.
* Supporting the Board of Trustees and Development Committee in advancing fundraising engagement.

**Key Qualifications**
* 5–7\+ years of progressive nonprofit fundraising experience with demonstrated success meeting revenue goals.
* Bachelor’s degree required; Master’s degree or equivalent experience preferred.
* Proven track record cultivating and securing major gifts from individuals, foundations, and corporations.
* Experience managing a personal donor portfolio and building a strong fundraising pipeline aligned to annual revenue targets.
* Experience implementing or contributing to a comprehensive fundraising strategy.
* Strong relationship\-building and communication skills.
* Experience managing donor databases and interpreting fundraising data.
* Highly organized with strong planning and project management abilities.
* Entrepreneurial mindset and ability to grow fundraising initiatives.
* Passion for improving quality of life and wellbeing for older adults.

**Preferred**
* Experience in healthcare, aging services, arts, or community\-based organizations
* Experience with planned or legacy giving programs.


  

**Compensation**  

Starting Salary of $100,000\+, commensurate with experience, plus a competitive benefits package.
  

**About Goddard House**  

Goddard House is a nonprofit organization with more than 175 years of service to older adults in the Greater Boston community. Located in Brookline, Massachusetts, Goddard House operates an innovative assisted living and memory care community with 115 re

jobs.title: Chief Operating Officer
jobs.description:
**Job Summary**

We are seeking a dynamic and strategic Chief Operating Officer (COO) to lead our organization's operational functions and drive sustainable growth. The COO will oversee business operations, implement operational excellence initiatives, and manage large\-scale organizational change. This role requires an energetic leader with a proven track record in executive leadership, strategic planning, and business development management. The ideal candidate will excel in fostering a culture of continuous improvement, data\-driven decision making, and operational efficiency to maximize profitability and support long\-term organizational success.

**Duties**

* Develop and execute comprehensive operational strategies aligned with the company’s vision and goals, ensuring scalable growth and operational excellence.
* Lead business development management efforts to identify new opportunities, expand market presence, and strengthen client relationships.
* Oversee business operations across departments, ensuring seamless coordination, process improvement, and adherence to quality standards.
* Manage operational budgets, including forecasting, financial management experience, profit and loss analysis, and executive\-level budget management.
* Drive large\-scale organizational change management initiatives to optimize workflows, enhance productivity, and foster a culture of innovation.
* Implement operational analysis techniques to monitor performance metrics, identify areas for improvement, and execute continuous process improvements.
* Collaborate with senior leadership to develop strategic plans, set objectives, and ensure alignment across all levels of the organization.
* Supervise project management activities to ensure timely delivery of initiatives that support business growth and operational efficiency.
* Communicate effectively with executive teams through verbal communication with executives to present insights, updates, and strategic recommendations.
* Lead management efforts by supervising teams at various levels, promoting leadership development, and ensuring high\-performance standards are met.

**Experience**

* Extensive experience in operational management within a fast\-paced organizational environment with demonstrated success in leading large teams.
* Proven track record in implementing operational excellence initiatives that improve efficiency and reduce costs.
* Strong background in business development management with experience in identifying growth opportunities and expanding organizational reach.
* Demonstrated expertise in change management processes during large\-scale organizational transformations.
* Financial management experience including budgeting, forecasting, profit and loss analysis, and operational analysis.
* Exceptional leadership skills with experience managing executive\-level teams and fostering a collaborative work environment.
* Proficiency in project management methodologies with a focus on process improvement and strategic planning.
* Excellent verbal communication skills tailored for engaging with executives and stakeholders at all levels.
* Data\-driven decision\-making capabilities supported by analytical skills to inform strategic initiatives.

Join us as our Chief Operating Officer if you're ready to lead transformative change, inspire teams at every level, and shape the future of our organization through innovative operations management!

Pay: $196,031\.14 \- $257,693\.41 per year

Work Location: Remote

new_jobs.title: Senior Director of Development
new_jobs.description:
**Senior Director of Development**
==================================


We are seeking an accomplished and results\-driven **Senior Director of Development** to lead the organization's fundraising, business development, and revenue\-generation strategy.


This senior leadership position is responsible for expanding and diversifying funding through corporate partnerships, major gifts, grants, sponsorships, and national and global initiatives. The Senior Director of Development will exercise a high level of independent judgment in identifying revenue opportunities, cultivating relationships, negotiating partnerships, and developing strategic initiatives that advance the organization's mission and long\-term financial sustainability.


The ideal candidate is a strategic relationship builder with a demonstrated record of securing significant funding, developing strong donor and corporate partnerships, and leading revenue\-focused development efforts within a nonprofit or mission\-driven organization.

**We offer the following competitive benefits package:**

* Competitive Salary based on experience
* Medical Benefits
* Paid Time Off
* Paid Holidays
* Retirement Plan

**Why Join Alma Domestic Violence Foundation?**
===============================================


At **Alma Domestic Violence Foundation (ADVF)**, we believe every individual deserves to live free from violence, fear, and abuse. Our mission is to empower survivors of domestic violence by providing comprehensive advocacy, supportive services, housing resources, legal assistance, education, and community partnerships that promote safety, healing, and long\-term independence. As our **Senior Director of Development**, you will play a pivotal role in advancing this mission by securing the financial resources and strategic partnerships needed to expand our programs and increase our impact. Your leadership will directly support innovative initiatives that improve the lives of survivors and strengthen communities throughout Metro Atlanta and beyond. This is more than a fundraising position\-it is an opportunity to shape the future of a growing nonprofit organization, cultivate transformational partnerships, and help build sustainable funding that allows ADVF to serve more individuals and families in need. If you are a visionary leader with a passion for philanthropy, community engagement, and creating lasting social change, we invite you to join our team and make a meaningful difference.

**Position Overview**
=====================

**Fundraising and Revenue Strategy**
====================================

* Develop and execute a comprehensive development and revenue strategy aligned with the organization's mission, strategic priorities, and financial goals.
* Identify, cultivate, solicit, and secure funding from corporations, foundations, government entities, major donors, and other funding partners.
* Lead sales\-oriented fundraising strategies designed to generate sustainable revenue and expand organizational impact.
* Establish annual fundraising goals, performance benchmarks, and revenue projections.
* Analyze fundraising performance and adjust strategies as needed to meet financial targets.

**Business Development and Strategic Partnerships**
===================================================

* Build and manage a robust pipeline of prospective donors, sponsors, corporate partners, and funding organizations.
* Lead partnership discussions, sponsorship proposals, and funding negotiations.
* Establish and maintain strategic relationships with corporate, philanthropic, governmental, and community partners.
* Identify opportunities to expand the organization's presence through national and international partnerships and initiatives.
* Represent the organization at conferences, networking events, donor meetings, community engagements, and other professional forums.

**Donor Engagement and Stewardship**
====================================

* Develop and oversee donor cultivation, solicitation, recognition, engagement, and stewardship strategies.
* Strengthen relationships with existing donors while identifying opportunities to increase giving and long\-term engagement.
* Ensure donors and funding partners receive timely communication, impact updates, recognition, and reporting.
* Support the development of compelling funding proposals, sponsorship materials, presentations, and donor communications.

**Grant and Funding Compliance**
================================

* Collaborate with executive leadership and program teams to align funding opportunities with organizational and programmatic priorities.
* Ensure compliance with donor requirements, grant conditions, contractual obligations, and reporting deadlines.
* Monitor the appropriate use of restricted funds and support accurate financial and programmatic reporting.
* Maintain organized records of funding opportunities, donor interactions, proposals, awards, and reporting requirements.

**Leadership and Team Management**
==================================

* Provide strategic direction and leadership to development staff, consultants, volunteers, and other assigned personnel.
* Establish performance expectations, monitor progress, and promote accountability for development goals.
* Support the professional development and effectiveness of the development team.
* Foster a collaborative, ethical, and results\-oriented fundraising culture across the organization.

**Required Qualifications**
===========================

* Bachelor's degree in business, nonprofit management, communications, public administration, marketing, or a related field.
* At least 7–10 years of progressive experience in fundraising, business development, corporate partnerships, nonprofit development, or sales leadership.
* Demonstrated success securing major gifts, corporate sponsorships, grants, or other significant funding.
* Proven experience building and managing donor or business development pipelines.
* St

new_jobs.title: Executive Director
new_jobs.description:
**About Us**

Latimer House is a nonprofit historic house living museum dedicated to preserving and sharing the history and culture of our community. Through tours and special events, we connect visitors of all ages with our region's unique heritage.

**Position Summary**

The Executive Director serves as the museum's chief executive and is responsible for overall leadership, administration, financial management, fundraising, and community engagement. Reporting to the Board of Directors, the Executive Director works closely with staff, volunteers, donors, and community partners to advance the museum's mission and ensure long\-term sustainability.

**Key Responsibilities**

* Lead the day\-to\-day operations of the museum.
* Develop and manage the annual budget.
* Oversee financial reporting and organizational compliance.
* Build relationships with donors, members, sponsors, and community partners.
* Lead fundraising efforts, including grants, corporate and individual donations and special events.
* Supervise staff and volunteers.
* Work with the Board of Directors on strategic planning and governance.
* Oversee museum tours, exhibits, create, implement and oversee programming, and visitor experience.
* Ensure proper care of museum facilities and collections.
* Represent the museum at community events and public meetings.

**Qualifications**

Required:

* Bachelor's degree or equivalent experience.
* Experience with CRM.
* Experience managing budgets and personnel.
* Strong written and verbal communication skills.
* Demonstrated fundraising or grant\-writing experience.
* Ability to work collaboratively with volunteers and community stakeholders.

Preferred:

* Experience in museums, historic sites, or cultural organizations.
* Knowledge of nonprofit governance.
* Experience with membership programs and donor relations.
* Familiarity with collections management or public programming.

**Ideal Candidate**

We're looking for a collaborative leader who is organized, adaptable, and passionate about local history. The ideal candidate enjoys wearing many hats, solving problems creatively, and building relationships throughout the community.

**To Apply**

Please submit:

* Cover letter
* Resume
* Three professional references

Resumes will be reviewed beginning 7/20/2026 and will remain open until the position is filled.

**Latimer House / Lower Cape Fear Historical Society, Inc. is an Equal Opportunity Employer.**

Pay: From $40,000\.00 per year

Work Location: In person
